label: Linear models & generalized linear models

node.label: Linear models & generalized linear models
node.definition: Linear and generalized linear models predict through a weighted sum of features, optionally passed through a link function chosen for the target distribution.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Linear models & generalized linear models is modeled as depending on Affine transforms & dense layers, Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy and feeding downstream nodes such as Recommendation, ranking & personalization, Medical imaging & clinical decision support, Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
examples: atlas downstream link to Recommendation, ranking & personalization
atlas downstream link to Medical imaging & clinical decision support
atlas downstream link to Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Affine transforms & dense layers, Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy.

dependencies.rationale: Linear models & generalized linear models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.

In this atlas graph, Linear models & generalized linear models has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy because Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.

In this atlas graph, Linear models & generalized linear models has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust) because Regression losses (MSE, MAE, robust) is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
Linear models & generalized linear models follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
